description: The noun naming what we eat to sustain life, the nourishment that becomes our bodies. In Phi, food is understood as gift and transformation—what was living becomes our living, what grew from earth becomes our growth. This word names both the physical substance we consume and the quality of nourishment, of being fed, of receiving what sustains. Food is how the world enters us and keeps us alive.
sound_symbolism: The word opens with 'nu', where the nasal grounds us in the body that needs feeding—hunger is felt, eating is physical, nourishment is embodied. The deep 'u' holds the substantiality of food. The standalone 'o' opens into receiving, mouth open to be fed. The final 'ra' rolls with the continuous process of eating, digesting, being nourished—the ongoing transformation of food into self. The arc traces nourishment: bodily need, receiving mouth, continuous sustaining.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun referring to food generally or specific foods. Often appears in expressions of eating, hunger, satisfaction, and gratitude. Can be used with meal-related vocabulary.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Sustainable food systems are foundational. 'Nuora' names what we must grow, gather, share, and eat in ways that can continue—food sovereignty, local production, fair distribution.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional food was local, seasonal, prepared with skill passed through generations. 'Nuora' honors food as more than calories—as culture, as relationship with land, as accumulated wisdom.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Mindful eating transforms routine consumption into practice—awareness of what we eat, how it came to us, what it becomes in us. 'Nuora' names the substance of this practice.

tags.nature: the gifts of earth that nourish us
